utility_notes: preserve PM2.5 regression signal without using target pm2.5 in theta
DEWP, TEMP, PRES, Iws, hour, month, and cbwd are the strongest utility-side feature candidates
PM2.5 is right-skewed; high-pollution episodes depend on seasonal, meteorological, and wind-regime structure
utility evaluation metric in the existing pipeline is RMSE (lower is better); describe utility qualitatively rather than claim new experiment results

pair_priors: dew point and temperature jointly encode humidity and air-mass conditions linked to PM2.5
temperature-pressure structure reflects seasonal and synoptic weather regimes
wind direction and cumulative wind speed jointly control pollutant dispersion
diurnal temperature cycles interact with hourly pollution patterns
seasonality drives temperature distribution and PM2.5 regime shifts
seasonality also controls humidity/dew point conditions relevant to pollution episodes
hourly wind accumulation and boundary-layer cycles affect dispersion
seasonal pressure regimes are important weather anchors
snow and rain indicators are sparse precipitation features with related zero-inflated behavior
calendar structure should remain coherent across the multi-year time series
exact timestamp granularity can create unique temporal records and should stay coherent
wind direction and temperature together describe weather regimes

shape: Is and Ir are strongly zero-inflated sparse precipitation variables; synthetic data can easily drop rare nonzero events
Iws is long-tailed with high extremes; preserving only central mass may miss strong wind episodes
DEWP, TEMP, and PRES have seasonal distributions that should not be flattened
cbwd has only four categories but uneven frequencies; wind direction mix should remain plausible
trend: DEWP, TEMP, and PRES must remain physically coherent as weather variables
cbwd and Iws jointly describe wind regime and pollutant dispersion
hour and month interact with weather variables through diurnal and seasonal cycles
sparse precipitation variables Is and Ir should remain consistent with weather regimes
privacy: exact year/month/day/hour combined with weather can create near-unique records
Iws and pressure/temperature values can act as quasi-identifiers when paired with exact time
DEWP is currently configured as privacy-sensitive and should remain prominent in col_ps
broad privacy scope is useful, but do not include all feature columns because theta validation caps col_ps below full feature count
utility: dropping meteorological anchors such as DEWP, TEMP, PRES, or Iws weakens PM2.5 regression utility
using only calendar columns for utility is too weak; weather and wind columns carry the main regression signal
overemphasizing sparse Is/Ir as col_u is risky because they are mostly zero
target pm2.5 must never appear in theta even though it is the regression target

seed_theta_examples.reason: emphasizes marginal fidelity for DEWP/TEMP/PRES/Iws plus sparse Is/Ir precipitation tails; col_ps omits exact year/day so it is a weather-and-precipitation privacy scope rather than a reordered timestamp scope
covers meteorological and wind-regime dependencies from pair priors: DEWP-TEMP, TEMP-PRES, cbwd-Iws, hour-TEMP, month-DEWP, and year-month seasonal coherence

privacy-aware seed uses the broadest Beijing col_ps: exact timestamp granularity with DEWP/TEMP/PRES/Iws, cbwd wind regime, and Ir rare-weather signal; it omits Is so the DCR combination remains distinct

utility-aware seed follows real regression signal where DEWP, Iws, cbwd, PRES, month, TEMP, and hour dominate PM2.5; col_ps keeps utility/weather anchors but differs from both trend and privacy scopes
